'''Logan Moy''' (born 10 August 2005) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Betfred Super League]]. 

He previously spent time on loan from [[Hull FC|Hull]] at [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|accessdate=8 January 2026|url=https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/logan-moy-61039.html|website=it's rugby|title=Logan Moy}}</ref>

==Background==
Moy was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England.<ref>{{cite web|accessdate=8 January 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/an-ultimate-13-of-rugby-league-players-born-in-hull|title=An ultimate 13 of rugby league players born in Hull, featuring FC and KR stars aplenty|website=Love Rugby League|first=Ben|last=Olawumi|date=5 Apr 2025|publisher=Planet Sport }}</ref> He attended Malet Lambert School.<ref name=nextgen>{{cite web|accessdate=8 January 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-logan-moy-hull-fc-super-league|title=NextGen: Meet the Hull FC fullback tipped for Super League stardom who coaches 'fell in love with'|first=Ross|last=Heppenstall|date=25 Jul 2024|website=Love Rugby League|publisher=Planet Sport}}</ref> 

He played for the [[Skirlaugh Bulls]] as a junior.<ref name=nextgen/> Moy joined [[Hull FC|FC]]'s Academy system in 2022<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/logan-moy-opens-up-hull-9266821|title=Logan Moy opens up on his Hull FC debut, position tweaks and what comes next|first=Daniel|last=Tomlinson|date=7 May 2024}}</ref> and was the Academy player of the year in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|accessdate=8 January 2026|url=https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2023/12/29/moy-discusses-life-in-first-team-squad/|title=Moy Discusses Life In First-Team Squad|date=29 December 2023|website=Hull FC blog}}</ref>

==Career==
===Hull FC===
Moy moved into the Hull FC first team squad ahead of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fcs-first-team-hopefuls-10733061 |title=Hull FC’s first-team hopefuls detailed as new crop aim to follow trio’s mark|first=Daniel |last=Tomlinson|date=3 Jan 2026}}</ref> He made his professional debut for Hull FC in April 2024 against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[2024 Super League season|Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw00d7kg95no|title=Full-back Moy extends Hull FC contract until 2027|website=[[BBC News]]|date=24 May 2024}}</ref> He signed a three-year contract extension with Hull in May 2024.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Yorkshire Post|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/really-exciting-moment-hull-fc-tie-down-young-gun-logan-moy-4641490 |title='Really exciting moment': Hull FC tie down young gun Logan Moy| first=James|last=O'Brien|date=24 May 2024}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC (loan)===
Moy spent time in 2025 on loan from Hull FC at [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[2025 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|accessdate=8 January 2026|url=https://www.totalrl.com/bradford-bulls-leave-it-late-to-overcome-stubborn-doncaster/ |title=Bradford Bulls leave it late to overcome stubborn Doncaster|first=Christian|last=Lee|date=8 August 2025|website=Total Rugby League}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers (loan)===
On 6 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Mar 2026|accessdate=7 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Halifax Panthers secure loan additions from Hull FC and Wakefield Trinity|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/halifax-panthers-hull-fc-wakefield-trinity}}</ref>
